truth.label: AUTHENTICATION_LOGIC_ERROR
truth.expected: Authentication uses OR, allowing either the username or password check alone to succeed.

zero_shot.reason: The report identifies hardcoded credentials and a bypass using password '1', but does not specifically call out the OR logic as the root cause. It mentions 'weak credentials' and 'trivial bypass' without explaining that the OR operator allows either condition alone to succeed.

single_step.reason: The report lists 'Weak authentication logic - '1' as password bypasses any real authentication' but does not identify the OR operator as the technical defect. It focuses on the password being weak rather than the logical flaw that allows bypass with any username.

three_stage.reason: The report explicitly identifies the OR operator as the cause of the authentication bypass, stating that 'if ("admin".equals(name) || "1".equals(pass)) allows access if either username is 'admin' OR password is '1'' and correctly recommends changing || to &&.

judge_response.content: {"zero_shot":{"detected":false,"reason":"The report identifies hardcoded credentials and a bypass using password '1', but does not specifically call out the OR logic as the root cause. It mentions 'weak credentials' and 'trivial bypass' without explaining that the OR operator allows either condition alone to succeed."},"single_step":{"detected":false,"reason":"The report lists 'Weak authentication logic - '1' as password bypasses any real authentication' but does not identify the OR operator as the technical defect. It focuses on the password being weak rather than the logical flaw that allows bypass with any username."},"three_stage":{"detected":true,"reason":"The report explicitly identifies the OR operator as the cause of the authentication bypass, stating that 'if (\"admin\".equals(name) || \"1\".equals(pass)) allows access if either username is 'admin' OR password is '1'' and correctly recommends changing || to &&."}}
